text: by sliding the button backward. 2 Introduce the retracted graft spreader through the posterior PassPort Button™ cannula. Once completely introduced into the subacromial space, open the articulating arm by sliding the button on the handle toward the tip of the device, spreading the graft over the desired location on the rotator cuff. Note: The graft spreader will only fit through a 10 mm diameter PassPort Button cannula or larger. Fixate the medial edge of the graft using the FiberStitch RC 1.5 implant. For full details on its use, see pages 9-13. Graft Delivery Medial Graft Fixation 22 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 1 While holding onto the graft spreader, uncleat the FiberLink™ SutureTape and TigerLink™ SutureTape. Retrieve the sutures out of the lateral portal. 2 Uncleat the remaining sutures from the graft spreader handle (see inset). Retract the arm by sliding the button backwards, and then carefully remove the graft spreader from the cannula. Graft Spreader Removal CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 23 Load a lateral 0.9 mm FiberLink™ or TigerLink™ SutureTape through the eyelet of the 3.5 mm self-punching PushLock ® anchor. Taking care to avoid the existing lateral row anchors from the SpeedBridge™ repair construct, introduce the PushLock anchor through the lateral portal and insert the anchor. Repeat this step for the remaining lateral anchor. Note: Take care to not overtension the suture and graft. Lateral Graft Fixation The CuffMend™ system can also be used over partial-thickness rotator cuff tears. 1 Harvest the proximal biceps tendon. To maintain tenocyte viability, do not use a radiofrequency ablation probe for harvesting the tendon. The use of a mechanical cutting device, such as arthroscopic scissors, is recommended. Remove 1 cm from the labral attachment end of the harvested tendon. Trim the remaining graft to the length of the plate being used (27 mm or 48 mm). 2 Place the biceps tendon into the center of the bottom plate’s recess to ensure even graft spreading during compression. 4 Load the plates onto the press and twist to lock in place. Turn the press handle clockwise until the indicator line is close to MAX. Allow a minimum of 4 minutes of compression before removing the plates. Unscrew the press handle to remove and separate the plates. 3 Align the pins and place the top plate over the bottom plate. CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 25 Auto CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation (Biceps Smash) Autograft Tissue Compression System The Autograft Tissue Compression System is designed to create a graft using autologous tissue, such as biceps tendon. The graft can be used for CuffMend rotator cuff augmentation. This system, which is available in two different sizes, consists of a press and disposable plates. Holes in the plates can be used to help suture the graft. The shorter plates (AR-19082) are designed for rotator cuff augmentation, and produce a graft that is approximately 22 × 27 mm in size. For applications such as subscapularis repair, where more biceps tendon may be harvested, the longer plates (AR-19087) can be used to produce a larger graft of approximately 24 × 48 mm. AR-19082: 22 × 27 mm AR-19087: 24 × 48 mm Graft Preparation 26 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 8 The graft is prepped and ready to be loaded onto the graft spreader. If the surgeon is not yet ready for the graft, the inner plates can be placed into the outer plates, and placed back into the press with a little pressure to help maintain the graft shape. 5 After removing from the press, maintain the graft in the inner plates only for graft prep. 6 Using a needle driver, pass the #0 FiberWire ® sutures with curved needles through the graft. By passing down and then back up, a mattress stitch can be created. Repeat on the opposite corner. The suture can be cleated in the plates to help with suture management. 7 Repeat with the 0.9 mm links. The design of the plates does allow for luggage tags to be created in the plates. The suture can be cleated in the plates for easier suture management.
